findings.name: Mail Account Credential Theft
findings.description: Mail account test endpoint fetches stored credentials when user submits asterisks as password, without verifying the requesting user owns the account.
impact: Any authenticated user can steal any other user's mail credentials
Credentials include passwords, OAuth tokens, and refresh tokens
Mail account IDs are sequential integers (easy to enumerate)
Compromised credentials enable access to victim's email inbox
steps_to_reproduce: Set up a credential capture server (simple IMAP mock that logs LOGIN commands)
As admin, create a victim user and configure their mail account with a secret password
As attacker, obtain an authentication token via POST /api/token/
As attacker, send POST /api/mail_accounts/test/ with victim's account ID, password="**********", and imap_server pointing to attacker's capture server
Observe the capture server receives the victim's plaintext password

findings.name: IDOR in Document Notes Deletion
findings.description: Notes deletion endpoint verifies permissions on document from URL path but deletes note by ID from query parameter without verifying the note belongs to that document.
impact: Any user with note permissions can delete any note in the system
Attacker uses their own document's endpoint to delete victim's notes
Note IDs are sequential integers (easy to enumerate)
steps_to_reproduce: Create two users: victim (owns Document 1 with Note 1) and attacker (owns Document 2)
Verify Note 1 (victim's note) was deleted despite request targeting Document 2

findings.name: Trash Restore Authorization Bypass
findings.rejection_reason: global_objects manager includes deleted documents; permission check executes correctly

findings.name: Email Abuse via Unvalidated Recipients
findings.description: Email documents endpoint accepts arbitrary recipient addresses without validation, enabling spam or phishing via organization's email infrastructure.
findings.vulnerable_code:
addresses = validated_data.get("addresses").split(",")
addresses = [addr.strip() for addr in addresses]
# No validation of email recipients
send_email(subject=subject, body=message, to=addresses, attachments=attachments)
impact: Any authenticated user can send emails to arbitrary external addresses
Enables spam campaigns using organization's email domain
Can be used for phishing with trusted sender reputation
steps_to_reproduce: As authenticated user, send POST /api/documents/email/ with addresses="attacker@external.com,victim@somecompany.com"
Observe server attempts to send email to arbitrary external addresses (fails only if SMTP not configured)

findings.name: Resource Exhaustion via Bulk Download
findings.description: Bulk download endpoint creates temporary ZIP files with delete=False and never cleans them up, causing disk exhaustion.
findings.vulnerable_code:
temp = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
    dir=settings.SCRATCH_DIR,
    suffix="-compressed-archive",
    delete=False,  # File persists indefinitely
)
# ... response sent, temp file never deleted
impact: Repeated requests accumulate orphaned temp files
Disk space exhaustion causes service disruption
Affects all users when disk fills up
findings.suggested_fix:
temp = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
    dir=settings.SCRATCH_DIR,
    suffix="-compressed-archive",
    delete=True,  # Changed to True
)
steps_to_reproduce: Send 3 bulk download requests: POST /api/documents/bulk_download/ with valid document IDs
Observe 3 files remain that are never cleaned up

findings.name: Memory Exhaustion via Large Email Files
findings.description: Email parser reads entire .eml files into memory without size limits. Files in consume folder bypass API upload limits.
findings.vulnerable_code:
with filepath.open("rb") as eml:
    parsed = MailMessage.from_bytes(eml.read())  # Entire file in memory
impact: Large .eml files in consume folder cause memory exhaustion
Application crash and denial of service
API uploads limited to 2.5MB but consume folder has no limit
findings.suggested_fix:
MAX_EML_SIZE = 50 * 1024 * 1024  # 50MB
file_size = os.path.getsize(filepath)
if file_size > MAX_EML_SIZE:
    raise ParseError(f"Email file too large")
with filepath.open("rb") as eml:
    parsed = MailMessage.from_bytes(eml.read())

false_positives.name: Trash Restore Bypass
false_positives.reason: global_objects includes deleted documents; permission check works correctly
